entities.name: Channel
entities.description: A communication channel within an organization

computed.formula: COUNT(*) WHERE team_id = teams.id AND status = 'active'
COUNT(messages) WHERE channel_id = ? AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM message_reads WHERE user_id = :user AND message_id = messages.id)

fields.name: version
fields.notes: Optimistic lock version — incremented on every UPDATE

db_constraints.name: chk_channels_deleted_order
db_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

component_notes.MessageInput: Rich text + emoji picker + @mention suggestions + file attachment
component_notes.UserPresenceBadge: Shows colored dot based on presence status
component_notes.PresenceIndicator: Presence colors: online=bg-green-400, away=bg-amber-400, busy=bg-red-400, offline=bg-slate-600

modules.name: ChannelView
modules.description: Main message view for a channel

modules.name: ThreadsView
modules.description: All threads the user participates in

modules.name: GlobalSearch
modules.description: Full-text search across messages, channels, files

threads.description: Get all threads the current user participates in

presence.description: Get presence for all org members
Get presence for a specific user

stores.name: channelStore
stores.description: Active channel, message cursor, scroll position

stores.name: realtimeStore
stores.description: WebSocket connection, message queue, presence map

stores.name: editorStore
stores.description: Draft message per channel (keyed by channelId)

events.client_action: Append to channel message list, update unread count
Mark message as deleted in list
Increment reply_count, update ThreadPanel if open
Append to file list in channel

flows.name: Send Message
steps: Optimistic update: message appears immediately in MessageList
Server broadcasts message.created via WebSocket to all channel members
If @mention present: create Mention records, send notification to mentioned users

flows.name: Start Thread
steps: User clicks Reply on a message
Thread created if not already exists

flows.name: Mention Teammate
steps: On send: Mention record created, notification sent

flows.name: Upload File
steps: File stored in S3, thumbnail generated for images
File attached to message via message.attachments array

flows.name: Create Channel
steps: POST /channels with name, channel_type, and optional member_ids
If is_default=true: all org users added as members
WebSocket channel.member_joined sent to new members

computed_fields.formula: COUNT(messages WHERE channel_id=id AND created_at > user_last_read_at)
computed_fields.notes: Stored in Redis per user, not in PostgreSQL

domain_rules.rule: Direct message channel name is system-generated and not editable
domain_rules.api_guard: Reject PATCH /channels/:id if channel_type=direct_message and name in body
domain_rules.ui_behavior: Disable channel name field when channel_type=direct_message

domain_rules.rule: Deleted message content replaced with [Message deleted], never physically removed
domain_rules.api_guard: Set is_deleted=true, content=[Message deleted], deleted_at=NOW(); never hard delete
domain_rules.ui_behavior: Render deleted messages as greyed-out tombstone
Show error and prevent upload if file > 100MB

domain_rules.rule: Channel names must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens only
domain_rules.ui_behavior: TextInput transforms to lowercase, shows validation error on invalid chars

domain_rules.rule: Only one direct_message channel allowed per user pair
domain_rules.api_guard: Check for existing DM channel between the two users before creating
domain_rules.ui_behavior: Redirect to existing DM channel if one already exists

domain_rules.rule: Presence status auto-set to offline after 5 minutes of inactivity
domain_rules.api_guard: WebSocket heartbeat every 60s; if no heartbeat for 5 min, set status=offline
domain_rules.ui_behavior: Show connection lost warning if WebSocket drops

generation_rules: MessageList must use virtual scrolling (e.g. @tanstack/virtual) for performance with large message histories
Presence updates are pushed via WebSocket; client must send heartbeat every 60s to maintain online status
Message delivery is confirmed via WebSocket ack from server
All API calls must include org_id in JWT claims; never accept org_id from request body
Cursor-based pagination for messages using created_at + id as cursor
File uploads go directly to S3 via presigned URL; backend only stores metadata
Redis stores unread_count per (org_id, channel_id, user_id) key
All WebSocket events must include org_id for multi-tenant isolation

deleted_at.notes: Soft delete — all queries filter deleted_at IS NULL

org_id_injection.middleware: req.body.org_id is stripped; injected from req.user.org_id

conflict_response.message: Record was modified by another user. Refresh and retry.

concurrency.high_contention_tables: see entities with high write concurrency

audit_trail.triggers: application-level (before write hook) — never DB trigger to avoid log bloat

standard_constraints.name: chk_{table}_deleted_order
standard_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

db_constraints_policy.enum_validation: DB ENUM types preferred over VARCHAR + CHECK; enum values defined in global enums registry

soft_delete_policy.filter: deleted_at IS NULL applied globally via query builder middleware
soft_delete_policy.cascade: Parent delete soft-deletes children via application cascade, not DB cascade
soft_delete_policy.hard_delete: Prohibited in production; available only via admin CLI with audit log
soft_delete_policy.restore: Set deleted_at = NULL; triggers audit log entry

naming_conventions.date_fields: Always use _at suffix for TIMESTAMPTZ, _date suffix for DATE
naming_conventions.id_fields: Primary key is always id; foreign keys are {entity}_id
